taxonomy.title: Requirements / intent misalignment
taxonomy.definition: The agent misreads user intent, hidden constraints, or acceptance criteria, then optimizes for the wrong target.
taxonomy.whyItHurts: Creates valid-looking but irrelevant work, increasing rework and user-agent backtracking cycles.
typicalSignals: User says 'not what I asked' or 'wrong approach'
Late clarification changes solution direction
Implementation solves adjacent problem instead of requested one

taxonomy.title: Context acquisition / codebase navigation
taxonomy.definition: The agent fails to find or prioritize the right files, docs, APIs, or historical context in a large codebase.
taxonomy.whyItHurts: Produces navigation thrash (many reads/searches, few decisive edits) and increases the chance of acting on stale or incomplete context.
typicalSignals: Repeated reads/searches with little progress
Mentions docs are missing, outdated, or contradictory
Wrong-file edits or late file switching

taxonomy.title: Planning / search strategy failure
taxonomy.definition: The agent picks a low-quality plan (or no plan), explores weak branches too long, or fails to prune bad hypotheses early.
taxonomy.whyItHurts: Increases long-horizon drift and cost before reaching a viable solution path.
typicalSignals: Multiple abandoned approaches
Late pivot after many turns
Step sequences that do not reduce uncertainty

taxonomy.title: Tool orchestration / invocation errors
taxonomy.definition: The agent selects wrong tools, malformed arguments, wrong tool order, or misreads tool outputs.
taxonomy.whyItHurts: Causes avoidable failures, retries, and false confidence from incorrect observations.
typicalSignals: Same tool error repeats
Calls tools before required data is gathered
Argument mismatch and failed execution cycles

taxonomy.title: Code-change quality / patch correctness
taxonomy.definition: Edits compile or apply but are semantically wrong, incomplete, or fragile under edge conditions.
taxonomy.whyItHurts: Looks like progress until verification fails, then requires high-cost refactoring or rollback.
typicalSignals: Patch applies but tests still fail
Repeated fix attempts around same failure
Edge-case or integration regressions

taxonomy.title: Verification / testing strategy gaps
taxonomy.definition: The agent validates too late, with wrong tests, or without targeted checks for likely failure modes.
taxonomy.whyItHurts: Allows errors to compound, delaying detection of wrong tracks.
typicalSignals: Long edit sequence before first test
Repeated broad test runs with little diagnosis
Failed assertions recur without narrowed hypotheses

taxonomy.title: Control-loop / recovery failure
taxonomy.definition: The agent loops, retries ineffectively, or fails to recover from early mistakes (error propagation).
taxonomy.whyItHurts: Consumes rounds/tokens with diminishing returns and makes root-cause localization harder over time.
typicalSignals: Retry loops with near-identical failures
'Stuck' behavior and repeated narration
Late explicit realization of wrong path

taxonomy.title: Resource constraints (tokens, context, time)
taxonomy.definition: The agent degrades under context pressure, truncation, compaction, or long-duration sessions.
taxonomy.whyItHurts: Critical signals can be missed ('lost in the middle'), causing wrong decisions and expensive detours.
typicalSignals: Context length or truncation warnings
Compaction/context-overflow events
Large token spend before correction

researchBasis.title: SWE-bench (ICLR 2024)
researchBasis.claim: Real-world GitHub issue resolution is a challenging, multi-step task where even strong models struggled on realistic software engineering workflows.

researchBasis.title: SWE-Bench Pro (2025)
researchBasis.claim: Long-horizon coding tasks expose clustered failure modes such as wrong solution, tool-use errors, syntax errors, incorrect file targeting, context issues, and loop behavior.

researchBasis.title: Process-oriented trajectory analysis (2025/2026)
researchBasis.claim: Execution errors and debugging trajectories strongly correlate with lower resolution rates and higher reasoning overhead.

researchBasis.title: Lost in the Middle (2023)
researchBasis.claim: Models often underuse relevant information in the middle of long contexts, causing context-position-dependent failures.

researchBasis.title: ReAct (2022/2023)
researchBasis.claim: Interleaving reasoning and actions improves performance, but also highlights that tool-action loops are central to reliability.

researchBasis.title: Tool-calling hallucination detection (2026)
researchBasis.claim: Tool hallucinations include wrong tool choice, malformed parameters, and tool bypass; these require explicit runtime detection.

researchBasis.title: ToolCritic taxonomy (2025)
researchBasis.claim: Tool-use failures are multi-type (e.g., premature invocation, argument misalignment, output misinterpretation) and benefit from structured correction.

researchBasis.title: Agent error propagation (2025)
researchBasis.claim: Early root-cause mistakes frequently cascade into downstream failures; identifying first critical error is key.
